sections.title: 1. Purpose
sections.section_prompt: State that this Risk Management File documents the application of ISO 14971:2019 to {device_name_model} across its entire lifecycle, in conformance with ISO 13485:2016 §7.1 and §7.3.3, feeding design inputs ({ref:RD-002}) and post-market surveillance ({ref:RA-005}).

sections.title: 2. Scope
sections.content: Applies to all hazards, hazardous situations, and harms associated with {device_name_model} (intended use: {intended_use}) manufactured by {company_legal_name}, including normal use, foreseeable misuse, single-fault conditions, and end-of-life.

sections.title: 3. Risk Management Process (ISO 14971 §4)
clauses: 3.1 Risk management plan and acceptability criteria
3.2 Risk analysis: intended use, characteristics related to safety, hazard identification, risk estimation
3.4 Risk control: option analysis, implementation, residual risk evaluation
3.7 Production and post-production information (post-market feedback loop)
sections.section_prompt: For each clause, explain in 3–4 sentences how {company_legal_name} performs the activity for {device_name_model}. State the severity scale (e.g. 1–5), probability scale, and the acceptability matrix used. Reference the hazard register that follows.

sections.title: 4. Hazard / Risk Register
sections.ai_rows_prompt: Generate 12–20 realistic hazards for {device_name_model} covering: energy hazards (electrical, mechanical, thermal), biological/chemical (if patient contact), operational (use error, misuse), information (labelling, IFU), software/cybersecurity (if software), interoperability (if connected). Use ISO 14971 Annex C taxonomy. S and P on 1–5 scale. Residual S×P must always be ≤ initial. Verification Ref points to {ref:RD-005} test reports (e.g. TR-EMC-04) or design controls in {ref:RD-003}. Return JSON array.

sections.title: 5. Overall Residual Risk Evaluation
sections.section_prompt: Provide an overall residual risk statement for {device_name_model}: count of risks by residual zone (acceptable / ALARP / unacceptable), justification that overall residual risk is acceptable in light of clinical benefit, and reference to the benefit-risk analysis. Cite EU MDR Annex I 8 if EU market.

sections.title: 6. Production & Post-Production Information
sections.section_prompt: Describe how field data from complaints ({ref:CA-004}), nonconformances ({ref:CA-007}), CAPA ({ref:CA-001}), vigilance ({ref:CA-005}), and PMS ({ref:RA-005}) is reviewed at least annually and used to update this file.
